title: The Red Planet Potion
summary: A curious band of travelers, including Papa Mole and the scientific Uncle Grisco, visit the home of Seeker to begin a journey beyond the stars. After selecting a swirling multi-colored potion from a magical closet, the group recites an ancient spell to transport themselves to the surface of Mars. Shielded by a magic bubble of air, they explore the rocky Martian landscape, investigate massive impact craters, and observe the planet's unique pinkish sky before safely returning home using a special brew.

transcript.text: Yes?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: We're going to tell a secret story.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong!
Who is it? says Seeker.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: It's Papa Mole and the blind mices.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, hello Papa Mole and the blind mices. What brings you to my house today?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Actually, Papa Mole and Uncle- Uncle Grisco and the blind mice.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I see. Well, welcome to my house, says Seeker. Would you like to come in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.
Oh, okay. Come on in.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And my goodbye Papa Mole came in with them.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Uh-huh. Well, it's three of you, says Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's three of you, said Seeker, yes. Oh, poor Abby baby, she's crying.
She's, I think she's blowing big raspberries and they're crying.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, well, what would you like to talk about at my house today, Uncle Grisco and Papa Mole?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's, we want to go to- we want to explore.
The sun to our space planet land.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the sun to the outer space planet land.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Do you have a potion of that?
That's a good question, said Seeker. That is a very challenging...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I hope you can buy a new one.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Well, if I don't have one, maybe I could buy one. Yeah, that's a good idea.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Can you go check?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay, I'll check and see if I have any outer space potions or a sun potion. Hmm. I'll look in my potion closet. Wow, I have a lot of potions in here that I still need to try. Now, this one's all blue with sparkles...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Outer space is black.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, yeah, you're right. Now, this potion is black, but it's not got any twinkly stars in it. Oh, but look at this one. It's bright white and yellow, and it is shining and glowing with light. I think this might be a sun potion. But we should be careful. We don't want to get too close to the sun because it's really hot. Hmm.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Actually, we're not- they're not going to the sun today. Um, no, they're going to Mars.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, going to Mars.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Going to Mars, not the sun. And they're going to Mars and the potion, so it has to be red, twinkled with stars, black, and red, and orange and yellow for the volcano, and also red for the volcano.
Okay, red and orange and yellow and twinkled with stars and black. Hmm, I'll see.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And red for the volcano and also orange for the volcano and yellow for the volcano.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Seeker says, well, let me check my closet. Gosh, now this one has all those colors in it. Maybe this one will do it. It's got a beautiful swirl and spiraling mix of red and orange and yellow and black and it's sparkling.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And, and does it have stars?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: It has some little stars. I think this is our Mars potion. Okay, everyone gather round. And is Izzy the kiddo there too or just Papa Mole and Uncle Grisco?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just Papa Mole and Uncle Grisco.
And the blind mice, right.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: You forgot about them.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Can't forget about the blind mice. How many are there?
There's three blind mice, one Uncle Grisco, and one Seeker, and one Papa Mole.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: So six creatures all together. And they each took a sip of the Mars potion. Zip zip zip. And then they said, okay, someone has to say the magic spell.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Fiska Kapiska Kaboo!
Fiska Kapiska Kaboo! Third time, here we go.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Fiska Kapiska Kaboo!
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And there was a big zoooooom-pow! And when they opened their eyes, they were standing on the planet Mars. And they had a magical bubble of comfortable air to breathe all around the six of them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And did you bring the going home potion?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, yes, I've always got a bunch of going home potions in my cloak right here, so we never have to worry about that. Now, it looks like we made it somewhere. Let's look all around and see what we can see. What do you notice out there, everybody?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I noticed that the planet that we're on is Mars, is red like yellow and orangey too. Has a volcano-ish reddish Mars.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's where we got to.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I see a bunch of red rocks and dust all around, including right here under our feet. Look, we can stomp on the red Martian dirt.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Also, see any yellow for a volcano and red for the volcano and orange for the volcano.

Well, I don't think we're close to any volcanoes. I see some mountains in the distance and that pointy one might be a volcano, but it's not going to erupt anytime soon. We're safe from that. And the sky is different than the sky on Earth. What color is the sky on Earth?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: The sky on Earth is blue, but the sky in outer space is black.

That's right, and here on Mars the sky is kind of reddish-pink. It's a very strange sky. Hmm, and there's little wispy clouds all around. Mars is an interesting place because it's very different from Earth. And Mars is a smaller planet, so it has a little less gravity. So everyone feels a little bit lighter and you can jump higher. Boing!
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now, there's one other interesting thing that happens on Mars, and that is that there's wind and there's dust and that means sometimes there are dust storms. I think one's coming toward us now. It's when the wind blows lots of dust. Whoosh, whoosh! But we're safe from that because we're in our little bubble that gives us air we can breathe. Because Mars doesn't have air on it that people can breathe, but we brought our little magic bubble with us so we can breathe.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I brought an, I brought a water bottle.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's good, because there's no water on Mars to drink either.
We also have a sink right next door from my room.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's good, just in case we need to drink some more water.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I take baths in there every night.
Mmm, so nice to take baths.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: And, and I want to stay in the hot water all day sometimes.
Yeah, does it feel nice?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: So cozy to have a bath before bed, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I want to lay down for start- and stories.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay, well, I think we're almost done with stars and stories. Let's do one more thing on Mars before we go home, said Seeker. Do you want to find a crater or a Mars Rover robot or an alien?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Let's find a crater.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ah, okay, says Seeker. We're on just some regular rocky ground now, but I think there's a crater over there. If we all walk close together, we can move our air bubble along with us so we can safely walk across the surface of Mars. Okay, let's go this way. And so Seeker led the way and they all walked across the dirt and the rocks until they got to a ridge, which is like a little hill. And they walked over the ridge and down into a big circular hole in the ground. And Seeker says, well, we made it. This is the crater. Does anybody know what made this crater?
No.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And every answer was no, except Uncle Grisco, because he knows about science. He's a science teacher. And he said, oh, yes, craters are formed by impacts. That's when a rock flies down from outer space and hits a planet like Mars. And it sometimes- they go so fast and they hit it so hard that they make a big hole in the ground. And that hole is a big circle like this. And it's called a crater.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, that's very interesting. Are there any craters on Earth? What do you think?
There are no craters on Earth.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: None that we've seen ourselves, but there are some that you can go and visit from a long time ago when a rock hit the Earth. But we're safe from that. It almost never happens.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: Does the moon have craters?
Sometimes it does.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: The moon has lots of craters all over it. Yeah. And you can even see them when you look up at the moon from the Earth. Okay, everyone, that's enough Mars exploration. It's time to drink our going home potion, said Seeker. Glug, glug, glug. And everyone drank the going home potion and they went back to Seeker's house and said goodbye to Mars. And that's the end of our story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think this blanket made a really good stars, by the way.
Dad, can you do your phone magic?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh no, okay. Should I shut...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, let's do shadow puppets and you're going to be first to do shadow puppets and show me.
Okay.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: The shadow puppets that you make that you make.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, this one looks a little bit like a bird. I'm a bird. Chirp chirp chirp. You got to look at the shadow on the ceiling.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now I'm going to make mine. I'm a bunny, hop hop hop.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah, you're doing it.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, Dad, one more. Hand creature.
It's a hand creature puppet. Rawr rawr rawr. Okay.
transcript.speaker: Dad

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: The Three Blind Mice
characters.description: A group of tiny adventurers who accompany Papa Mole on the expedition.

places.name: Seeker's House
places.description: Perched at the edge of a mountain, this warm stone sanctuary—Seeker’s original residence and whimsical home shared with Uncle Crisco—has evolved from a secret vault into the purring, chaotic heart of a massive feline gala. Within its well-lit halls where Muppets gather for snacks and scientific discussions, lies a special hidden closet where Seeker brews an enchanted collection of potions that shimmer in every imaginable color.

places.name: Outer Space Planet Land
places.description: A distant region of the galaxy that contains the sun and various planets.

places.name: Mars
places.description: Serving as the home of Julian and the primary destination for the Floofers’ quest for the mookball, this primordial red world hosts the Red Bolt landing site. Defined by the rhythmic thunder of giant volcanoes and the jagged edges of ancient impact craters, the ash-shrouded landscape rests under a hazy pink sky in a haunting silence—a world devoid of other human life where only the fire and dust endure.

places.name: The Moon
places.description: Visible from Earth as a pockmarked sphere of gray, white, and black, this low-gravity waypoint for sun-bound crews is a playground of ancient space rocks and leaping Floofers; its silver dust now serves as a silent sanctuary for a misplaced book and the long-lost mother cat.
